macro_summary: Markets are navigating the most earnings-dense week of Q2 season with GOOGL and TSLA reporting July 22, INTC July 23, MSFT and FOMC on July 29, and AAPL on July 30. The Fed is widely expected to hold (~87-90% probability), which should remove the primary rate-shock headwind for growth multiples. The semiconductor sector has sold off sharply (~20% from SOX peak) while broader Nasdaq-100 remains more resilient, creating valuation dislocations in AI-adjacent names. HBM memory supply remains structurally tight through 2027 per management guidance, supporting AI infrastructure demand narratives heading into the print cycle.
screening_note: 12 overnight screening runs analyzed; minimum threshold of 2 appearances required to qualify. All 5 ideas cleared with strong repetition — SKHY appeared in all 12 runs and NVDA in 10, signaling unusually high algorithmic consensus.
key_events: MSFT earnings + FOMC rate decision: July 29, 2026
SK Hynix Korean share circulation event: July 29, 2026

ideas.name: SK Hynix Inc. (Nasdaq ADR)
ideas.thesis: SKHY appeared in all 12 overnight screening runs — the strongest consensus signal of the session. The ADR broke below its July 10 IPO price of $149 to ~$147, creating a post-IPO dislocation entry against a 12-month analyst consensus target of $342.50 (~+122% upside) and a Barclays Overweight initiation citing 70%+ upside. As the global HBM market-share leader with supply sold out through most of 2027 and a primary NVIDIA supply relationship, the structural demand thesis is intact; the current price weakness is technical and event-driven, not fundamental.
ideas.catalyst: Korean new share circulation event July 29, 2026 (potential short-term selling pressure and reset); SK Hynix Q2 earnings on Korean exchange expected late July / early August
ideas.edge: ADR at ~$147, below $149 IPO price, vs. 2-analyst consensus 12-month target of $342.50 (+122%); Barclays initiated Overweight with 70%+ upside; HBM supply contracted through most of 2027
ideas.bull_case: Post-IPO dislocation below IPO price historically creates mean-reversion setups; a Barclays Overweight initiation plus structural HBM shortage narrative should attract institutional buying once volatility stabilizes around the July 29 Korean share event.
ideas.bear_case: Post-IPO volatility remains extremely high on a new Nasdaq listing; July 29 Korean share circulation could accelerate selling pressure; options liquidity is still nascent, making defined-risk structures harder to execute at fair prices.

ideas.name: Invesco QQQ Trust (Nasdaq-100 ETF)
ideas.thesis: QQQ appeared in 11 of 12 overnight screens as the cleanest liquid vehicle to express either a directional or volatility thesis into the most catalyst-dense 48-hour window of the quarter. The July 29 FOMC decision (hold at ~87-90% probability) lands the same day as MSFT earnings, immediately followed by AAPL on July 30 — a compressed dual macro/earnings event stack that is historically associated with outsized index moves in either direction. NDX has only pulled back ~3% vs. SOX's ~20% decline, keeping the non-chip mega-cap recovery thesis structurally intact.
ideas.catalyst: FOMC rate decision July 29, 2026 + MSFT earnings July 29 + AAPL earnings July 30 — three major catalysts in a 48-hour window; GOOGL earnings July 22 as advance signal
ideas.edge: A QQQ straddle or strangle into the July 29-30 catalyst cluster captures potential outsized moves in either direction at defined cost; Fed hold consensus removes the primary rate-shock tail risk while leaving earnings surprise risk open
ideas.bull_case: A confirmed Fed hold removes the rate-shock headwind for NDX growth multiples; if hyperscaler AI capex guides hold firm across GOOGL/MSFT/AAPL prints, QQQ captures a broad tech recovery without direct semiconductor risk.
ideas.bear_case: If FOMC surprises hawkishly or hyperscalers disappoint on AI capex / Azure guidance, QQQ could sell off sharply; a September Fed hike probability of ~65% cited in some runs suggests rate risk is not fully priced out.

ideas.name: NVIDIA Corporation
ideas.thesis: NVDA appeared in 10 of 12 overnight runs with 9 high-conviction votes — the highest buy-side conviction score in this session. The stock has been sold down to ~$202 alongside the broader SOX decline, pushing its forward P/E to ~22x — near a decade low — despite 85%+ YoY revenue growth and a confirmed August 26 earnings catalyst with consensus revenue ~$91-93.5B. The ~$100/share gap to the 68-analyst average price target of ~$302 represents one of the deepest valuation dislocations in mega-cap tech right now, with a 5-week runway to the catalyst favoring options over stock.
ideas.catalyst: Q2 FY2027 earnings confirmed August 26, 2026 after market close; consensus revenue estimate ~$91.77–$93.5B, EPS ~$2.08–$2.12
ideas.edge: Stock at ~$202 vs. 68-analyst consensus target of ~$302 (+49% gap); forward P/E ~22x near decade low vs. 5-year average of 72x; EPS revisions 33 up vs. 3 down in last 90 days; 85% buy-side consensus Buy rating
ideas.bull_case: At a decade-low valuation multiple with a confirmed earnings beat track record and structural HBM/AI accelerator demand, the Aug 26 print could be the re-rating catalyst; a defined-risk call spread (Aug/Sep expiry) captures the pre-earnings run with capped downside.
ideas.bear_case: Elevated implied volatility raises the cost of options premium; if sector sentiment remains weak into the print or hyperscaler capex guides disappoint in the July earnings cluster, NVDA could remain under pressure through August.

ideas.name: Apple Inc.
ideas.thesis: AAPL appeared in 7 of 12 runs with a notably split directional signal — some runs see a dip-buy entry with stock near $283 vs. a $315 consensus target, while others flag the stock at ~$333, above the $318 analyst consensus, making it rich into the print. The key risk across both price references is the July 30 earnings date, where tariff cost commentary on the September quarter and gross margin trajectory are the binary swing factors. Services revenue ($30.98B, +16.1% YoY at 32%+ margins) provides structural support, but a Keybanc cut to $250 on July 14 signals real downside risk. Options-implied moves of 4-6% historically, with elevated IV into the print, favor defined-risk structures over outright stock.
ideas.catalyst: Earnings July 30, 2026 after market close — tariff guidance for September quarter is the key swing factor; Keybanc downgrade to $250 target on July 14 as pre-print signal
ideas.edge: Options-implied move historically 4-6% around print; elevated pre-earnings IV favors premium strategies (iron condor) or directional defined-risk spreads; $100B buyback provides downside floor; 8 consecutive EPS beats
ideas.bull_case: Eight consecutive earnings beats, a $100B buyback backstop, and Services revenue accelerating at 32%+ margins could drive a beat-and-raise scenario; any tariff cost relief or margin expansion commentary would be a positive re-rating catalyst.
ideas.bear_case: Stock may be trading at or above analyst consensus targets, Keybanc's $250 target implies material downside, and tariff-driven September quarter margin compression could disappoint; an iron condor or put spread is the preferred hedge against a miss.

ideas.name: Microsoft Corporation
ideas.thesis: MSFT appeared in 7 of 12 overnight runs with all 7 votes at high conviction — the cleanest directional conviction signal among the earnings plays. The stock at ~$401 carries a ~25% gap to BofA's $500 price target, and MSFT demonstrated relative strength on July 15 (up ~3% while semis cratered), signaling rotation into non-chip mega-cap tech. The July 29 earnings date coincides with the FOMC decision, creating a dual catalyst session where Azure growth rate and Copilot monetization are the key swing factors. GOOGL's July 22 print arrives first as an advance read on hyperscaler AI capex.
ideas.catalyst: Fiscal Q4 2026 earnings July 29, 2026 after market close — same day as FOMC rate decision; GOOGL July 22 print as advance AI capex signal; Azure growth rate and Copilot monetization as key swing factors
ideas.edge: Stock at ~$401 vs. BofA $500 price target (~+25% gap); demonstrated sector rotation tailwind (outperformed semis on July 15); FOMC hold consensus removes rate-shock risk on the same day as the print
ideas.bull_case: A confirmed Fed hold combined with strong Azure growth and Copilot monetization metrics could drive a significant re-rating from current levels; the $99 analyst target gap and sector rotation tailwind make a pre-earnings call debit spread an asymmetric setup.
ideas.bear_case: The July 29 FOMC-earnings collision creates a high-volatility single session where a hawkish Fed surprise could overwhelm a solid earnings beat; if Azure growth decelerates or Copilot monetization disappoints, the stock could sell off sharply despite the analyst target gap.
